description_long: This object depicts a small section of a graveyard, featuring two distinct grave markers set on a rectangular patch of ground covered with fallen leaves. The first marker is a dark, ornate cross with trefoil ends, likely made of wrought iron, standing upright and weathered. The second marker is a simpler, lighter-colored stone cross with a square base, showing signs of age and moss or lichen growth. The ground is densely layered with yellow and brown leaves, suggesting an autumn setting and adding a natural, textured surface. Both grave markers are spaced apart, with the iron cross appearing taller and more prominent than the stone cross. No visible inscriptions or text are present on either marker, and there are no additional structures such as mausoleums or gates in the scene.
description: A small graveyard section with two crosses—one ornate iron cross and one simple stone cross—set on a leafy ground.
description_view_0: Front view showing both the ornate iron cross and the simpler stone cross on a leaf-covered ground.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the stone cross and the profile of the iron cross, with the leafy ground extending behind.
description_view_2: Rear view with both crosses visible from behind, emphasizing the iron cross's silhouette and the stone cross's back.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the stone cross's base and the thin profile of the iron cross.
